rationale: Both candidates independently catch the one still-live GT concern (the async_chunk getattr default inconsistency, True vs False) and correctly confirm the qwen3_tts scoping and seq_len cleanup already resolve the other named GT items; neither raises the amy-why-3459 design question about moving model-specific logic out of serving. X pulls ahead on the latent gap check: it explicitly flags that the split now keys solely on total_num_scheduled_tokens and drops hidden-shape alignment, questioning multi-axis handling and grounding it with what the downstream fix (#4910) later did — exactly the gap the human reviewers missed. Y touches the same code but declares it 'correct' and only asks for a comment, missing the gap. Y also pads its findings with speculative, hard-to-verify asks (an unverifiable claim about which PR-description test command ran, a docstring check with no evidence one exists), diluting precision versus X's tighter, well-verified findings.
